subject: Gaming
title: Achievement Systems and Completionism
description: Learn Achievement Systems and Completionism in one minute.

hook.text: Some players spend 100 hours collecting every achievement. The game is long over but the checklist isn't.

buildup.text: Xbox introduced Gamerscore in 2005. PlayStation followed with Trophies. A meta-game was born.

discovery.text: Completionism taps into the need for closure. An incomplete list creates psychological discomfort.

twist.text: Some achievements require absurd tasks—find every collectible, die 1,000 times. Fun becomes obligation.

climax.text: Achievement systems turned gaming into two games: the real game and the meta-game of collecting points.

punchline.text: The game ended hours ago. The achievement list keeps you playing.

quiz.question: What psychological principle drives completionism in gaming?
options: The need for closure—an incomplete list creates psychological discomfort
